methodsAttempted.notes: Checked the official Taif University domain and robots.txt; public content pages outside disallowed paths were crawlable for the UAPT crawler user agent.

methodsAttempted.query: site:tu.edu.sa Taif University artificial intelligence policy OR generative AI OR ChatGPT OR academic integrity OR plagiarism
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located an official Taif University page for the Council of University Affairs 2025 AI guide, a plagiarism and academic-integrity page, and official graduate-studies AI assistant pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:tu.edu.sa جامعة الطائف الذكاء الاصطناعي النزاهة الأكاديمية حماية البيانات
methodsAttempted.notes: The Arabic version of the AI-guidance page supplied original-language evidence; the English version was treated as a helper translation only.

Followed official page links to the Arabic equivalent and to the linked SharePoint-hosted PDF preview. The PDF viewer exposed only preview chrome through Firecrawl, so it was not used as claim evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a Taif University Deanship of Graduate Studies and Scientific Research page in Arabic and English describing the Council of University Affairs 2025 guiding manual on AI use in universities and colleges. The Arabic page provided usable original-language evidence. A linked SharePoint PDF preview and a plagiarism page were documented but not used as evidence because the available crawl text was viewer-only or too thin for AI-policy claims.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-scoped claim about what Taif University's page says regarding the external Council of University Affairs AI guide; not approved as a binding university-wide Taif policy.
Approved as a narrow summary of topics named in the Arabic page. The wording avoids treating the guide as a mandatory Taif University policy.

artifacts.title: Taif University AI Guidance Source Review
artifacts.summary: Taif University's Deanship of Graduate Studies and Scientific Research hosts an Arabic page describing the Council of University Affairs 2025 guiding manual for AI use in governmental and private universities and colleges. The staged claims are limited to what the official Taif page says about the guide's purpose and covered principles, including responsible AI use, education and research quality, academic integrity, data protection, internal policies, digital security, and digital skills.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The source describes a Council of University Affairs guiding manual; the staged claims should not be promoted as a binding Taif University-specific AI policy without further official evidence.
The linked SharePoint PDF preview did not expose substantive PDF text during Firecrawl extraction and was not used as evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
